learning_objective: Students visualize exponential decay and predict remaining radioactivity after multiple half-lives.
specification:
Type: microsim **sim-id:** radioactive-decay
**Library:** p5.js
**Status:** Specified **Bloom Level:** Apply **Bloom Verb:** Calculate **Learning Objective:** Students visualize exponential decay and predict remaining radioactivity after multiple half-lives. **Instructional Rationale:** Abstract exponential decay becomes intuitive when students can manipulate half-life values and watch the curve change in real time. P5.js simulation showing a collection of 200 colored circles representing radioactive atoms. Each timestep, atoms have a probability of "decaying" (changing from bright green to gray). A slider controls the half-life (1-100 timesteps). A line graph on the right tracks the number of undecayed atoms over time, showing the characteristic exponential decay curve. Display the mathematical formula and current values. Play/pause button and reset button. Speed slider for animation rate.
